scenario.context: Station 01: network project for a new administration and service area with several follow-up tasks.
scenario.mission: You handle the kickoff task and several follow-up tasks from requirements through testing and handover.

station.profile: Requirements analysis, technical planning, rollout, testing, and portfolio classification
constraints: Consider economic efficiency and protection needs together
Justify technical decisions in a traceable way
Keep acceptance and operations in mind from the start

customerRequest.subject: Station 01: Network project for new administration area
customerRequest.body:
Please plan the base provisioning of network and central services for our new administration and service area.

We need a structured requirements and as-is/to-be view, key technical decisions, a rollout plan, and an approach for testing and handover.

questions.title: Set up project kickoff cleanly
questions.prompt: What is the most sensible first step for this network project?

options.text: Order components immediately so no time is lost
options.rationale: Without a clear need, that risks wrong purchases and rework.

options.text: Structure the customer requirements, user groups, services, and growth assumptions
options.rationale: That creates the basis for technical and economic decisions.

options.text: Only print the floor plan of the new area
options.rationale: A floor plan does not replace a proper requirements capture.

feedback.correct: Correct: The project needs a solid requirements picture first.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Without requirements, planning quickly becomes messy.

questions.title: Capture customer requirements
questions.prompt: Which information should definitely be gathered at the start? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Number of workstations and expected growth in the area
options.rationale: Affects segmentation, port demand, and scaling.

options.text: Critical services such as ERP, VoIP, or file storage
options.rationale: Determines priorities and availability requirements.

options.text: Only the client's preferred brand of switches
options.rationale: Brand preferences do not replace technical requirements work.

options.text: Security and access requirements for employees and guests
options.rationale: Important for network separation and protection needs.

options.text: Whether the case color matches the conference room
options.rationale: Has little relevance for the target network picture.

feedback.correct: Correct: The capture covers demand, services, and protection needs well.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: Please connect the business and technical requirements more strongly.

questions.title: From request to target state
questions.prompt: Put the first planning steps into a sensible order.
items: Capture customer requirements and boundary conditions
Analyze the current state and dependencies
Formulate the target state for networks and services
Prioritize implementation packages
Present an approval-ready high-level plan
correctOrder: Capture customer requirements and boundary conditions
Analyze the current state and dependencies
Formulate the target state for networks and services
Prioritize implementation packages
Present an approval-ready high-level plan

feedback.correct: Correct: The process leads methodically to the target state.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: First clarify need and current state, then prepare the target and approval.

questions.title: Port capacity with reserve
questions.prompt: In the new area, 38 PCs, 14 IP phones, 6 printers, and 4 access points are to be connected. How many switch ports are needed at minimum with a 15% reserve? Round up to whole ports.

feedback.correct: Correctly calculated.
feedback.wrong: Please account for all connections and the reserve correctly.

questions.title: Classify DevOps support
questions.prompt: Explain in 4-6 sentences how standardized configuration templates and a DevOps-like process can secure the rollout of this network project.
questions.expectedOutline: Reference to repeatable configuration, traceability, and low-risk rollout.

feedback.correct: Correct: The role of standardized provisioning is explained clearly.
feedback.wrong: Please connect DevOps, standardization, and rollout benefits more clearly.

questions.title: Prioritize the as-is analysis
questions.prompt: Which survey is most important for the as-is analysis?

options.text: Only note the manufacturer of the existing switches
options.rationale: Without technical details, that is only of limited use.

options.text: Write the final VLAN plan right away
options.rationale: That would lack the necessary basis without an as-is picture.

options.text: Capture cabling paths, existing segments, services, bottlenecks, and dependencies
options.rationale: This is exactly what leads to realistic migration and expansion decisions.

feedback.correct: Correct: The as-is analysis must make technical and operational dependencies visible.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Without a solid as-is analysis, the target state remains speculative.

questions.title: Prepare the requirements specification
questions.prompt: Which points belong in a solid requirements specification for this project? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Project goals, boundaries, and framework conditions
options.rationale: Creates clarity about scope and limits.

options.text: Expected services and required availability
options.rationale: Important for the network and server concept.

options.text: A fixed product model without justification
options.rationale: That belongs more in the technical solution than in the pure need view.

options.text: Security and access requirements
options.rationale: A relevant part of protection needs.

options.text: Vague statements like 'has to be somehow fast'
options.rationale: Vague statements do not help with planning or acceptance.

feedback.correct: Correct: The requirements specification stays at the need and target level but is still clear enough for implementation.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: Please structure demand, availability, and protection needs more clearly.

questions.title: Planning by company size
questions.prompt: Put the reasoning path for a suitable network design from small to large into order.
items: Classify site size and number of users
Record critical services and redundancy needs
Derive network segments and capacities
Size the hardware and service concept appropriately
Document scaling reserves
correctOrder: Classify site size and number of users
Record critical services and redundancy needs
Derive network segments and capacities
Size the hardware and service concept appropriately
Document scaling reserves

feedback.correct: Correct: This turns the company view into a solid plan.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: First consider business demand, then technical derivations and reserves.

questions.title: Follow-up task 1
questions.prompt: IT management approves the high-level concept. Now the key technical decisions for protocols, components, and WAN connectivity are to be specified in more detail.

questions.title: Define the network baseline
questions.prompt: Which technical direction makes the most sense for this administration area?

options.text: A TCP/IP-based network with clean segmentation and clearly defined service zones
options.rationale: This is sustainable for administration, security, and scalability.

options.text: Place all systems unfiltered into one flat segment
options.rationale: That makes security, troubleshooting, and growth harder.

options.text: Only think about network separation after go-live
options.rationale: That would be far too late for planning and protection needs.

feedback.correct: Correct: Segmentation and clear service zones make sense for operations.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: The technical direction must secure security and scaling early.

questions.title: Technical core components
questions.prompt: Which components are especially relevant for the target state? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Managed switches with VLAN and monitoring capability
options.rationale: Supports segmentation and operational transparency.

options.text: Firewall or router with clear rule handling and site connectivity
options.rationale: Important for WAN, protection, and inter-site links.

options.text: Random home routers without central management
options.rationale: A poor fit for enterprise requirements.

options.text: Central services for DHCP, name resolution, and authentication
options.rationale: These services create order and operational readiness.

options.text: An undocumented test device as the only core component
options.rationale: Not viable for a productive rollout.

feedback.correct: Correct: The selection accounts for network operations and service integration.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: Please connect the core network, protection, and central services more clearly.

questions.title: Estimate WAN bandwidth
questions.prompt: For the site, 84 employees are assumed with a peak load of 3.5 Mbit/s per person. What total load in Mbit/s should be planned at minimum?

feedback.correct: Correctly calculated.
feedback.wrong: Please multiply the number of users and the load per person correctly.

questions.title: Justify the server and service concept
questions.prompt: Describe in 4-6 sentences how you would think about server hardware, server software, and availability together for this area.
questions.expectedOutline: Connection between service role, hardware basis, and availability requirement.

feedback.correct: Correct: The interaction of hardware, service role, and availability is explained clearly.
feedback.wrong: Please connect server role, services, and availability more concretely.

questions.title: Validate the technical selection
questions.prompt: Put the path to a sound technical solution into order.
items: Clarify how protocols and services relate to the requirements
Weigh components and server roles against each other
Define WAN and site connectivity
Define implementation test criteria
Have the technical solution proposal approved
correctOrder: Clarify how protocols and services relate to the requirements
Weigh components and server roles against each other
Define WAN and site connectivity
Define implementation test criteria
Have the technical solution proposal approved

feedback.correct: Correct: The technical selection moves cleanly from need to approval.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please structure demand, selection, testing, and approval more consistently.

questions.title: Follow-up task 2
questions.prompt: Purchasing asks for a short assessment of how economic efficiency, security, and sustainability should influence the technical selection.

questions.title: Combine economics and protection needs
questions.prompt: Which aspects should be considered together in this assessment? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Operating costs across the planned service life
options.rationale: Not only the purchase price matters.

options.text: Protection needs for personal and business data
options.rationale: Security requirements influence architecture and component choice.

options.text: Energy demand and expandability of the components
options.rationale: Sustainability and future viability belong in the overall assessment.

options.text: Only the lowest purchase price
options.rationale: Short-term cheap solutions can become expensive in operations.

options.text: Only the visual impression in the rack
options.rationale: That has hardly any technical value for the decision.

feedback.correct: Correct: The assessment connects cost, protection, and sustainability in a sensible way.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: Please think more strongly about lifecycle, protection needs, and energy.

questions.title: Justify the procurement decision
questions.prompt: Which decision is the most plausible for the new area?

options.text: Choose a cheap device without monitoring and security functions
options.rationale: Saves money in the short term but creates operational risks.

options.text: Choose a slightly more expensive, energy-efficient model with central management and security features
options.rationale: Combines operational reliability, sustainability, and manageable cost.

options.text: Base the decision solely on the supplier's current stock
options.rationale: That ignores the professional requirements of the project.

feedback.correct: Correct: This decides not only cheaply but sustainably.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: The decision has to include lifecycle and protection needs.

questions.title: Calculate energy savings
questions.prompt: A switch stack planned so far needs 610 W, while the optimized variant needs 470 W. At 2200 operating hours per year and EUR 0.32 per kWh, what is the rounded annual saving in EUR?

feedback.correct: Correctly calculated.
feedback.wrong: Please combine the power difference, operating hours, and electricity price correctly.

questions.title: Follow-up task 3
questions.prompt: After the strategic approval, the solution is to be turned into an implementation specification and prepared as a controllable rollout.

questions.title: From requirements specification to implementation specification
questions.prompt: Put the steps for a clean transfer into the implementation specification into order.
items: Review demand and boundaries from the requirements specification
Specify functional and non-functional requirements
Describe technical implementation packages
Formulate acceptance criteria
Submit the implementation specification for approval
correctOrder: Review demand and boundaries from the requirements specification
Specify functional and non-functional requirements
Describe technical implementation packages
Formulate acceptance criteria
Submit the implementation specification for approval

feedback.correct: Correct: The implementation specification is derived cleanly from the underlying need.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please think from the need through specification to approval.

questions.title: Formulate requirements in measurable terms
questions.prompt: Explain in 4-6 sentences how you would separate functional and non-functional requirements for this network project and make them measurable.
questions.expectedOutline: Distinction between service content and quality criterion with measurable wording.

feedback.correct: Correct: The separation and measurability are explained clearly.
feedback.wrong: Please separate functional and non-functional requirements more clearly and formulate them measurably.

questions.title: Secure the rollout plan
questions.prompt: Which points must be included in the rollout plan? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Time windows, responsibilities, and approval points
options.rationale: Creates commitment for implementation.

options.text: Fallback options for critical steps
options.rationale: Reduces deployment risks.

options.text: Test and retest points before final acceptance
options.rationale: Secures quality before release.

options.text: No documentation so that the team stays flexible
options.rationale: That would be problematic for project handover and operations.

options.text: Only the purchase invoice as the sole project document
options.rationale: That is nowhere near enough for the rollout.

feedback.correct: Correct: The rollout plan is structured in a controllable way.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: Please secure coordination, fallback options, and testing together.

questions.title: Identify an acceptance criterion
questions.prompt: Which wording works as a useful acceptance criterion?

options.text: The network has to work well overall
options.rationale: Too vague for a clean acceptance process.

options.text: Users should be as satisfied as possible
options.rationale: A sensible goal, but not measurable enough.

options.text: All 52 workstations receive a valid address within 60 seconds after login and access to the approved services
options.rationale: Measurable, traceable, and directly testable.

feedback.correct: Correct: This criterion is clear, measurable, and testable.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Acceptance criteria have to be specific and measurable.

questions.title: Follow-up task 4
questions.prompt: To close the work, prepare testing, documentation, handover, and a brief classification of virtualization and managed services.

questions.title: Test and measurement plan
questions.prompt: Which points belong in a solid test and measurement plan? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Check the reachability of central services and segment boundaries
options.rationale: Covers core functionality and network separation.

options.text: Define measurement points, target values, and documentation format
options.rationale: Only then do results become traceable.

options.text: Classify deviations and remaining items
options.rationale: Helps with approval and rework.

options.text: Discuss errors only verbally and do not record them
options.rationale: That would not be viable for acceptance and operations.

options.text: Postpone measurements until after the project has ended
options.rationale: Testing has to happen before the final approval.

feedback.correct: Correct: The test plan is professionally and organizationally robust.
feedback.wrong: Partially wrong: Please secure target values, remaining items, and traceability more strongly.

questions.title: Measurement-point success rate
questions.prompt: Of 61 planned measurement points, 58 passed without findings. What is the rounded success rate in percent?

feedback.correct: Correctly calculated.
feedback.wrong: Please relate the passed measurement points correctly.

questions.title: Present virtualization briefly
questions.prompt: Describe in 4-6 sentences how you would explain to the customer the benefit of virtualization and managed-service levels for further expansion.
questions.expectedOutline: Customer-oriented classification of flexibility, operational support, and value proposition.

feedback.correct: Correct: The benefit is explained in a technically sound and customer-friendly way.
feedback.wrong: Please connect virtualization, managed-service levels, and customer benefit more concretely.

questions.title: Handover to operations and support
questions.prompt: What is most important for the handover after a successful rollout?

options.text: Only briefly announce that the project is finished
options.rationale: Without documentation and remaining items, operational stability is missing.

options.text: Hand over configurations, measurement results, remaining items, and escalation paths in a cleanly documented form
options.rationale: Secures operations, support, and rework in a traceable way.

options.text: Do not pass on the project documents at all just to be safe
options.rationale: That would massively complicate the handover into operations.

feedback.correct: Correct: A robust handover is part of project quality.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Without a clean handover, operations and support are slowed down.
